reviews.review_msg: great professor
When you search up mid in a thesaurus her name pops up
Dr. Lim is a nice prof but her final exam was much harder than expected. Expects a lot of detail for assignments

She kept lectures interesting and expectations on assignments and due dates were very clear. I would recommend reading and studying the textbook because she does incorporate it a fair amount, but overall a solid professor.

She has good lectures and the slides are easy to follow. The slides are accessible if you are not able to attend class. However, the exams are textbook heavy and can be difficult if you don't read the textbook.

Dr. Lim was fantastic. As a psych/crim major who has no science background, I found the class fair and not that difficult - but it does require a little more effort than the average psych class. She is highly organized, writes fair exams, and she is a passionate and engaging lecturer. Read the textbook if you want an A on exams. I highly recommend!

While I haven't been attending lecture a lot, but she makes it very easy to access slides outside of class. I didn't even touch the textbook, and the exams so far were relatively easy, so far as you go over the slides properly. Her assignments are also quite fun, and helped me remember content.

This was Dr. Lim's first time teaching PSYC 280, and she did very well. Lectures are also recorded. Her lecture slides are clear, and if you take notes and read the textbook, you will do well. Assignments are fun and silly. One of the midterms had a low avg and she dedicated a whole class to improving our grade (mine went up about 4%).

I really enjoyed Dr. Lim's lectures, she was very engaging and I felt that her teaching pace was good. Compared to other profs her slides are done really well and are easy to read (helpful for me as a visual learner). The exams aren't too difficult but you do need to read the textbook (if smth won't be included she will specify). Overall great!
Felt like I needed to know the textbook by heart. The quizzes are open-book. Tests rely on the textbook, and are worth a majority of your grade. Final grade doesn't round up.

I ws skeptical cuz of the reviews but Dr. Lim turned out to be an amazing prof. Her lectures were clear, engaging, & the material clicked. You do need to know most of the textbook, but she covers mostly everything in class. Lectures are recorded, weekly quizzes are open book. I'd definitely take this course again!
Purposely did not wear my seatbelt on the highway while driving to this class because I rather get into an accident than come to this class. Don't take this course.

3 exams worth 75% of the final grade, which can be difficult to do well if you do poorly on one of the exams; the averages for each of the exams were quite low. A very passionate professor, besides the difficult exams, this was actually my favourite class because of the content - it was super interesting. Reading the textbook highly recommended.
amazing prof, engaging lectures. exams are a bit tough. read the textbook!! she doesn't go over some modules in the slides but still uses them for exams

Not that tough of a class, social science students just get a lil shellshocked by the bio content. if you've taken any 1st year science courses or highschool bio you'll be fine.  

Exams absolute cakewalk if you have any science background. If you know what an ion is and how an action potential works your set.

Diana is kind but she doesn't know how difficult her exams are because they rarely reflect what was taught in class. She is approachable but the sheer amount of work and detail that is needed for this class is undermined. Midterm 2 class average was 56%, TA's were not responsive like not a single response to an email  all semester

Lectures were interesting and engaging and assignments/quizzes were straightforward for the most part.  Exams were tricky, make sure you study and understand material from the textbook as well as what she goes over in class.

I should of gotten my PHD in biopsyc before taking this class. Whatever was taught in class or in the textbook did not feel like it reflected on the exam questions. Some questions on the exam felt like material from a completely different class that is unrelated to what was learnt. I should of started studying while I was unconscious in the womb.
Good lecturer, cares about what she's talking about. Unfortunately you're gonna have to read the whole textbook to be able to do the exams :(

She's very polite and makes lectures interesting and memorable, but the speed at which she talks makes it difficult to write notes and pay attention at the same time. I've found that reading the textbook teaches more in depth but it is very time consuming and material from it is only sometimes in exams. Only assignment was a written assignment.

I relied on the multiple choice in the exams since it's 80% of the exams which is why I got a good mark (Currently at 92). Diana did a great job explaining concepts from the textbook, but you still need to read it!! MCQ is supposed to trick you a little so make sure you pay attention to small details unfortunately. Took a chunk of my time

Dr. Lim is a very engaging professor and she clearly loves psychology, however her quizzes, exams, and assignments are all just material from the textbook. TAs were always changing office hours and there was an online quiz every weekend, but we had Fri-Sun to complete it (10 MCQ in 12mins).

She is actually a good professor, despite what others are saying. But I'm not going to lie, her exams are tough. It's a very heavy course...and this class is my worst mark but also my favourite class cause I learned so much. TAs were lowkey useless, and marks were based on 75% (3 total) exams, 15% written assignments and 10% online quizzes.
Need to know the whole textbook. Helpful professor but the class is hard

She's honestly not a bad prof, for psyc 100 all she really did was summarize what was in the textbook, but even then when you have your weekly quiz you would need to read that whole chapter plus the lecture material to get a good mark. 

Wasn't all that engaged in class, unless there was an activity, it was hard to engage with material.
Exam averages with really low for a psyc course - and she doesn't advocate for students when TA's aren't fair with assignments.
exams were hard, and TAs didn't help especially with assignments.
Shes a good prof and her lectures are interesting, kinda entertaining. The exams are not too bad pretty straight foward from the lecture slides and textbook.
A lot of homework. Grading is tough depending on TA. They expect very specific answers so pay attention to the rubric. i barely made it but super do-able. Pretty strict grading system too.

Her lectures were engaging and fairly entertaining. She gives fair warning for all upcoming assignments with clear instructions. However, the tutorials felt like a waste of time; we were always let out super early and felt like there was nothing to talk about that wasn't said in the lecture already.

Lim's lectures are clear and easy to follow but man are her lectures boring / disengaging. Her TA's can be unreliable and inconsistent with grading, it seems like the prof and TA's are not on the same page. Doesn't seem like she really cares tbh. The TA's do not prepare you for the research paper adequately, so stay on top of it yourself.

Dr. Lim, while an engaging lecturer, is not an understanding professor. She does not provide accommodations regardless of documentation or circumstances on assignments. She can often be blunt and seems to expect her students to work as if they are machines. Her exams are heavily based on the textbook and difficult. Would avoid if possible!

She's a wonderful lecturer and person. The only thing is that she expects the whole textbook to be known, regardless of whether or not she mentioned certain topics in class. Other than that she's wonderful

I liked Diana she was nice but her exam material was tough. You better read the entire textbook front to back to do well on weekly quizzes and midterms. It is not easy with her, you have to work hard and make this class your top priority to keep up w/ weekly quizzes and 3 midterms. She also would not bump me 0.3% to have an A- in her class
lectures went over all the basic concepts from the textbook which was very helpful. responds to emails fast. nice lady. paper was graded pretty tough but depends on your TA

Most boring class ever, you MUST read the textbook and memorize literally all of it or you won't do that good. The average was extremely low. This prof and class isn't anything special, the content isn't too hard but this class just has so much info and it's so boring that it makes it hard. The wording on her exams is also weird.

Pretty straightforward prof, she tells you what she expects out of you and as long as you stick to readings and lectures youll be fine, doesn't try to trick you and was graded on 4 exams and one paper, as well as weekly quizzes. Shes a really nice prof super understanding and open to offer help.

The class material was very easy but she had some ambiguous questions on the exam where either answer are correct and you must choose the best answer which honestly you could argue is subjective. There's three exams and one essay which is graded very tough and I felt the guidelines were not very clear for the essay content. Lots of weekly reading.

Dr. Diana Lim is an amazing prof. I was a little iffy about taking her class cuz to the mixed reviews but she is a really caring teacher. Her lectures cover most of the main ideas but you should read the textbook to get the nitty gritty details. There are weekly quizzes based off the content covered that week and exams are straightforward.

Kind and encourages questions. Exams are very related to the textbook which is where most of your time is spent for the course. Questions on the exam were pretty fair assuming you properly read through the textbook and took notes on the key ideas.

She had engaging lectures that had you interact and brainstorm, which I appreciated. She seemed very passionate about the subjects that were discussed and took the time to answer our questions. Her exams were not very difficult and focuses mainly on the big ideas. She had weekly quizzes, which could give more credit! 

I would definitely take again

not great prof.didn't use a specific word they wanted on an exam and got no marks for the question. the material isn't hard to learn, its the expectation they have that make it impossible to do well even though its intro psyc. quizzes and exams are hard if you dont read EVERY word of the textbook you won't do well. very low class average

Diana was a great prof, no complaints with her in terms of explaining concepts and her lectures were also recorded. Only issue was the significant weight the textbook held on exams and quizzes, so you do have to read it in order to excel (and it is EXTREMELY dry). Other than that, the course is manageable as long as you keep up with readings:)
Very clear criteria and well organized. Exams and quizzes are tough, but is marked fairly. Her lectures cover the overall topics, but encourages us to read the textbook as well.

The professor is decent at best. The lecture covers basic concepts and is hard to sit through at times. Must read/review the whole textbook to get a good grade. The exams/quizzes are doable but made to trick you. The professor is strict and does NOT set up students to succeed. If Psyc 102 is a requirement take it with a different professor.

Great at teaching core concepts for the class, all of the assessments were very fair. The TA's were very nice and helpful, but their marking was surprisingly difficult. On two of my exams, I had the right idea but lost marks because I didn't use certain words. Otherwise solid class.

I think you're missing out if you don't take the course with her. Office hours were helpful, lectures were well organized and weekly quizzes weren't hard. I genuinely want to take another one of her classes.

There are a few things to note when taking her class. If you have tutorial at 8:30am, you have to hand in your research assignments in person, on campus, at 8:30am. She was not clear that we had to pay for iclicker subscription either. Her late penalty is fair and she is easy to talk to, she is just unclear on important course aspects

Since 201 is a 4 credit class it is a lot of work, but manageable if you stay ahead. I liked Dr. Lim as she was helpful, caring and knowledgable. Weekly i-clicker tests weren't too bad, tougher final exam than I thought it was going to be for multiple choice. Good class and prof.

I took 2 of Lim's classes and they are always well organized, canvas is kept up to date and there are no surprises. TAs seem to care. 70% of exam Qs are multiple choice. She does weekly low-stake canvas quizzes that are helpful to self-check how youre doing. Do those consistently and read the weekly chapter (~30 pages) and you'll get a B
